title: GEMM-Oriented Emulation of FP Matrix Multiplication Using Integer Modular Arithmetic
preview: Shows how to emulate floating-point matrix multiplication accurately using integer arithmetic units via modular techniques, enabling FP-quality results on integer-only hardware.
summary: Proposes an algorithm that decomposes floating-point GEMM into a sequence of integer operations using Chinese Remainder Theorem (CRT) and modular arithmetic. Achieves near-IEEE accuracy while targeting integer Tensor Cores and DSPs that run at higher efficiency than FP units.
datacenter: High relevance to hardware-software co-design: enables next-generation accelerators to provide FP32-equivalent accuracy using INT8 or INT4 Tensor Core paths, improving FLOPS/W and FLOPS/mm² for inference clusters.
metrics: Key result signal: accuracy parity with IEEE FP32 GEMM at significantly higher throughput using integer hardware.
